text:
Once upon a time in Elmshire, there lived a mysterious fox named Flash. Everybody liked Flash. Nobody knew how he knew everything.

One sunny afternoon, Flash found Rainbow Ridge. It was alive with animals of every color. He listened to their tales. A purple tortoise told of ocean adventures. A bluebird sang happy tunes. A leopard spider spun tales.

"I’ve heard whispers," muttered Flash. Maybe he knew more.

He stumbled upon a circle of butterflies. They fluttered nervously.

"A mystery," thought Flash.

He joined them.

"I've wings," he began.

They listened.

"I dream."

Their eyes widened.

"You dream of harvest."

Flash smiled wryly.

"To harvest dreams."

Later, he visited Owl creek. Its owner knew secrets.

"I hear whispers."

Owl nodded.

"Why?"

"He steals dreams."

"You don?"

"He teaches."

That night, Flash dreamed flowers that sang.

"You know," he whispered softly.

One grew.

"I know friendship."

Flash smiled.
